title: CVC SMT Solver Family
summary: A chain of three papers describing the evolution of the CVC family of SMT solvers. CVC4 adds competition-grade theory solvers (bit-vectors, non-linear arithmetic, arrays) with a fully rewritten SAT core (2WL, CDCL, restarts) and Nelson-Oppen theory combination. CVC4Sy extends CVC4 with syntax-guided synthesis (SyGuS) capabilities including CEGIS, grammar normalization, and multi-function synthesis support. cvc5 introduces proof production (Alethe format), theories of finite sets and sequences, quantifier instantiation (MBQI), incremental solving, and optimization.
instructionPreview: Implement a simplified SMT solver in Python that evolves through three stages, corresponding to three papers in the CVC solver family.
instruction:
Implement a simplified SMT solver in Python that evolves through three stages,
corresponding to three papers in the CVC solver family.

The working directory is /workspace. The solver framework is in solver/.
Base framework provides QF_UF (uninterpreted functions) and QF_LIA
(linear integer arithmetic) support with a basic CDCL SAT solver.

Implement the following modules in order:

1. CVC4 Competition Solvers: Rewrite SAT core with two-watched-literal
   propagation, VSIDS with phase saving, geometric restarts, and clause
   database reduction. Add QF_BV (bit-vectors) theory solver with eager
   bit-blasting, QF_NIA (non-linear arithmetic) with interval narrowing,
   array theory solver with lazy lemma instantiation, and Nelson-Oppen
   theory combination procedure.

2. CVC4Sy SyGuS: Add syntax-guided synthesis with CEGIS loop,
   grammar-constrained bottom-up enumeration with observational equivalence
   pruning, grammar normalization passes (unreachable removal, chain
   elimination, commutative reduction, constant folding), divide-and-conquer
   decomposition, and multi-function synthesis with dependency analysis.

3. cvc5 Features: Add proof production (Alethe format) with DAG-based proof
   builder and checker. Add QF_FS (finite sets) theory solver with membership
   propagation. Add sequence theory solver. Add quantifier instantiation
   (E-matching + MBQI). Add incremental solving with push/pop. Add
   optimization modulo theories.

Each module has benchmarks in benchmarks/. Run python3 run_benchmarks.py
to test. All benchmark results are output as KEY: VALUE lines.

Tests verify: QF_BV_CORRECT, QF_NIA_CORRECT, SYGUS_LIA_CORRECT,
QF_FS_CORRECT, PROOF_CORRECT (and regressions on earlier logics).

moduleDag.description: A chain of three papers describing the evolution of the CVC family of SMT solvers. CVC4 adds competition-grade theory solvers (bit-vectors, non-linear arithmetic, arrays) with a fully rewritten SAT core (2WL, CDCL, restarts) and Nelson-Oppen theory combination. CVC4Sy extends CVC4 with syntax-guided synthesis (SyGuS) capabilities including CEGIS, grammar normalization, and multi-function synthesis support. cvc5 introduces proof production (Alethe format), theories of finite sets and sequences, quantifier instantiation (MBQI), incremental solving, and optimization.

nodes.label: CVC4 at the SMT Competition 2018
nodes.description: Rewrites SAT core with proper two-watched-literal scheme, VSIDS with phase saving, geometric restarts, and clause database reduction. Adds bit-vector theory solver with eager bit-blasting, non-linear integer arithmetic solver with interval narrowing, array theory solver with lazy lemma instantiation, and Nelson-Oppen theory combination procedure.

nodes.label: CVC4Sy for SyGuS-COMP 2019
nodes.description: Adds syntax-guided synthesis via CEGIS with grammar-constrained bottom-up term enumeration, observational equivalence pruning, divide-and-conquer decomposition, grammar normalization passes (unreachable removal, chain elimination, commutative reduction), and multi-function synthesis with dependency graph analysis.

nodes.label: cvc5: A Versatile and Industrial-Strength SMT Solver
nodes.description: Adds proof production framework with Alethe-like output format and verification. Implements theory of finite sets with membership propagation, theory of sequences with bounded enumeration, quantifier instantiation (E-matching + MBQI), incremental solving with push/pop assertion stack, and optimization modulo theories.

edges.label: CVC4Sy extends CVC4 with synthesis strategies
cvc5 builds on the successful code base of CVC4
